question: In Adobe Character Animator, how do you activate the work area to get in-point and out-point markers on the timeline?
answer: First, click on the Timeline area. Then, right-click on the timeline to open the context menu and select the 'Enable Work Area' option, which activates the in-point and out-point markers.
keypoints: The user clicks on the 'Timeline' area within the Adobe Character Animator interface, where a blue playhead is visible.
The user right-clicks on the timeline at the top of the screen, revealing a context menu with options like "Enable Work Area," "Set Work Area Start to Playhead," and others.
The user selects the "Enable Work Area" option from the context menu, which activates an in-point and out-point on the timeline.

question: In Adobe Character Animator, what is the complete process to preview a specific hand movement sequence using the work area?
answer: Enable the work area via the timeline's context menu, drag the in-point/out-point markers to isolate the hand movement sequence, trim the scene to the work area, then press play to preview only that segment.
keypoints: The user selects the "Enable Work Area" option from the context menu, which activates an in-point and out-point on the timeline.
The user drags the in-point and out-point markers on the timeline to select a specific section of the animation that includes hand movements.
The user right-clicks again on the timeline and selects the "Trim Scene to Work Area" option, resulting in only the selected animation segment remaining in the project.
The user presses the play button located below the preview window, and only the trimmed animation plays, showing the character's hands moving as intended.
